resources.string: Select the app interface language. The change takes effect immediately after the screen restarts.
Configure API keys and models for AI providers. DeepSeek, OpenAI and others are supported. Without a key, the app cannot process requests.
Roles define the system prompt — AI behavior. You can create multiple roles with different personalities and switch between them in chat.
Enable web search so AI can find up-to-date information online. Google Custom Search, Tavily, and built-in DuckDuckGo (no key required) are available.
Customize theme (light/dark/system), Material You dynamic colors (Android 12+), and accent color.
Proactive mode automatically reminds you of calendar events, low battery, and important notifications. Quiet hours and priority threshold can be configured.
Manage AI long-term memory: user facts, daily entries, export and import data. Memory helps AI better understand context.
Configure scheduled tasks — daily reports, monitoring, reminders. Tasks run in the background.
Install external skills from the store or via URL. Skills extend AI capabilities: weather, news, home control.
Configure voice control: wake word for activation, speech recognition (Vosk), speech synthesis (TTS).
View agent logs in real time. Useful for debugging and monitoring AI operation.
Tap the microphone to input a message by voice. Recognition runs on-device via Vosk.
Take a photo or select one from the gallery. AI can analyze the image and describe it.
Attach a file: image, document, audio, or code. AI supports analysis of various formats.
Use the code copy button for quick copying. Code can be saved to a separate file.
Facts are key information about you that AI remembers. You can add, edit, and delete facts manually.
Export memory to Markdown for backup or transfer to another device. Import supports the same format.
An API key is required to access the AI provider. You can obtain a key from your provider dashboard. Keep your key secure.
Generate an API key on the DeepSeek API keys page: https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys
Generate an API key on the OpenAI API keys page: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
The AI model name used to process requests. Check the latest models on the provider\'s website.
DeepSeek models and pricing: https://api-docs.deepseek.com/quick_start/pricing#model-details
List of available OpenAI models: https://platform.openai.com/docs/models
Base URL for the API endpoint. Usually auto-filled when selecting a provider, but can be changed for custom endpoints.
